summary: The suite maps cleanly to all six stated requirements—INI parsing, sections, comments, continuation lines, type coercion, schema validation, JSON/YAML serialization, end-to-end pipeline, and file-not-found error handling—giving solid breadth. TDD iteration structure is faithfully reflected in the Describe/Context hierarchy, BeforeAll/BeforeEach fixtures are used appropriately, and coercion tests correctly assert both value and type. Deductions: the YAML quoting assertion uses `[''"?]?` making the quotes optional, so the test would pass even if quoting were omitted entirely; case-insensitive boolean variants (TRUE, YES, FALSE, NO in mixed case) are untested despite the implementation advertising (?i); no test for negative numbers; no test for schema validation when an entire required section is completely absent from data (only missing keys within a present section are checked); no AfterEach/AfterAll cleanup of fixture files, which can leave state between runs. These gaps mean a few meaningful regressions could slip through undetected, but the overall structure and coverage are above average.
